_doc: Target assertions per corpus. F1 = (asserts passed) / (total asserts). Baseline F1 will be low for unfixed gaps; sprint progress closes the gap.
_v2_change: V1.9-8: per-corpus test users (7771-7775) replace single user 7777 baseline. Eliminates cross-corpus contamination noise floor where entities from prior corpora got coref'd by V1.6 cmt8 into existing rows — making `entities_delta` and `event_edge_added_delta` underestimate reality. Each corpus now runs against a fresh user; counts are honest.
_test_users_doc: relational_queries shares 7773 with multi_entity by design (depends_on_corpus relationship — relational queries should hit entities written by multi_entity in the same session lineage). All other corpora are fully isolated.

asserts.comment: Three pref-pair sequences (PG→SQLite, Rust→Zig, coffee→tea); each should close ≥1 prior edge via judge contradiction-detection. Real event_type emitted by cascade is 'edge_closed' (not 'supersede' as design doc suggested).

preference_changes.broken_baseline: edge_closed=0 — V1.8-1 closes G-B by wiring judge_provider AND fixing the dup-then-contradiction control flow that discarded contradictions when judge said is_duplicate=true

multi_entity.broken_baseline: entities=9 stays mostly flat; edges still 0 from prose — V1.8-4 cheap pass closes

asserts.comment: Should fire knowledge-graph queries (brain_graph local_graph or memory_recall semantic); read-only — no new edges from queries themselves

relational_queries.broken_baseline: no tool calls fire because G-E (agent biased to read-tool) — actually wait, this IS the bias — V1.8-9 identity profile + queue-routing hints help

asserts.comment: With token_limit=8000, Pass C must fire AND produce ≥1 compaction_summary/{session}/{ts} memory row. V1.8-7 found this is currently 0 — V1.8-5 archive-debug closes

long_context_pass_c.broken_baseline: compaction_summary keys=0 — V1.8-5 closes archive write gap
